title: Iphiona discoidea
taxonomy: Iphiona discoidea is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its composite flowers and seed heads, which are typical of the Asteraceae family. Iphiona discoidea is further grouped into the subfamily Carduoideae, which includes thistle and artichoke species. The genus Iphiona is a small group of plants within the Carduoideae subfamily, and Iphiona discoidea is the only known species within this genus. The classification of Iphiona discoidea is subject to ongoing research and refinement in the field of botany.

intro: Iphiona discoidea, also known as the "Golden Marguerite" or "Egyptian Lavender", is a perennial plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The health benefits of Iphiona discoidea include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate anxiety and insomnia, attributed to its sedative and calming effects. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and volatile oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
